data.title: Arista Networks in spotlight as Wall Street likes 'robust' Q1, but tariffs, uncertainty weigh
data.description:
Arista Networks (NYSE:ANET) was in the spotlight on Wednesday after Wall Street analysts praised the networking company's stronger-than-expected first-quarter results, while simultaneously highlighting concerns related to tariffs and the global economy. 

Shares fell 7.5% in early trading.

Morgan Stanley analyst Meta Marshall, who has an Overweight rating and $100 price target on Arista, said the company is “undervalued” as it strongly positions itself in the $16B artificial intelligence networking market. 

“We walk away from Q1 encouraged by upside the company saw, even as deferred revenue balance grew, as [the] company continues to grow business in enterprise, cloud and in AI, providing meaningful growth runways for years to come,” she wrote in a note to clients. “We were most encouraged in the quarter by commentary that they are starting to see speed refreshes on the front-end of cloud, as share in the front-end of cloud is larger than in the back-end. While the company is certainly giving themselves some breathing room in the back-half to account for any pull forward or impact tariffs may have to demand, we think that is a better approach than we have seen from many names in the AI capex universe.”

J.P. Morgan analyst Samik Chatterjee said the results were “robust” and while the company is typically conservative with its guidance, its outlook for the fiscal second-quarter was “more robust” than anticipated.

“However, in typical conservatism, which is not entirely surprising for the management team, it is choosing to maintain the previously issued full-year guidance for +17% revenue growth, implying that 2H25 revenues would be similar to 1H25 despite both strong Cloud momentum as well as AI revenue ramp with multiple large Tier 1 and Tier 2 customers into the back half of the year,” Chatterjee wrote. “Management cited the uncertain macro and watchpoints in relation to how the current tariff dynamics unfold as the key reason to limit its own enthusiasm for now, despite citing strong visibility with Cloud customers into the entirety of 2025 as well as into a robust indication for 2026.” 

Chatterjee reiterated his Overweight rating and $110 price target.

Bank of America analyst Tal Liani reiterated his Buy rating and $130 price target and said the results were “solid” even if the guidance was its typical conservative nature.

“Revenue strength is expected to continue into 2Q with 24.3% YoY growth guidance vs. consensus’ 20.0%,” Liani wrote. “However, tariff uncertainties drove an implicit decline to 2H guidance, as we believe some of the [first-half] strength could be somewhat attributed to early ordering ahead of tariff-related price increases. Putting aside the tariff impact, we believe Arista proves repeatedly that it can successfully compete with white box switching and Nvidia’s solutions, with a stable position within its key Cloud Titan customers, and a leading position with Baby Clouds and Enterprises.”

data.title: Ford hikes prices on Mexican-made vehicles
data.description:
In reaction to increased import tariffs on its vehicles manufactured outside the U.S., Ford (NYSE:F) is raising the price of three Made-in-Mexico models, distinguishing itself as the first American automaker to compensate for changes in U.S. trade policy.

Consumers will now pay as much as $2,000 more for the Mustang Mach-E, Maverick pick-up, and Bronco Sport, according to a notice sent to Ford (NYSE:F) dealerships viewed by Reuters. The price hikes will impact vehicles built in Mexico after May 2 and arriving at dealerships in late June.

“The price hikes reflect usual mid-year pricing actions combined with some tariffs we are facing,” said a Ford spokesperson quoted by Reuters, adding that the company is not passing along the full cost of tariffs onto customers.

The decision comes on the heels of Ford’s (NYSE:F) first quarter results, at which time the company warned that tariffs will hit the bottom-line by as much as $2.5B (net $1.5B). The uncertainty surrounding trade policy coupled with anticipated disruptions to supply chains and consumer reaction to higher prices also led the company to pull 2025 guidance.

data.title: Disney sets eyes on Abu Dhabi for its next theme park resort
data.description:
The Walt Disney Company (NYSE:DIS) has planned for its seventh theme park resort in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates, the company said Wednesday.

The media and entertainment conglomerate has tied up with local partner Miral for the waterfront resort, which will be located on Yas Island, which is also home to Ferrari World, SeaWorld, and Warner Bros. World. 

Disney will lead creative design and operational oversight for the project, and Miral will develop, build, and operate the new resort.

“Our resort in Abu Dhabi will be the most advanced and interactive destination in our portfolio," chairman of Disney Experiences Josh D’Amaro said.

The new theme park resort will offer "signature Disney entertainment, themed accommodations, and unique dining and retail experiences" once completed, the companies said.

Financial terms for the proposed project were not disclosed.

Disney currently operates theme park resorts in California, Florida, Paris, Tokyo, Hong Kong, and Shanghai.

data.title: Awayday announces strategic investment by Ares Management and Lightbay Capital
data.description:
• Awayday said on Wednesday that an Ares Management Private Equity fund (NYSE:ARES) had made a strategic investment in the company.
  • The existing financial sponsor, LightBay Capital fully realized its investment in Fund I and re-invested in the company alongside Ares in Fund II.
  • Terms of the investment were not disclosed.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Theriva Biologics announces pricing of $7.5M public offering
data.description:
• Theriva Biologics (NYSE:TOVX) on announced the pricing of its “reasonable best efforts” public offering for the purchase and sale of up to 6.81M shares of common stock and warrants to purchase up to 6.81M shares of common stock at a combined offering price of $1.10 per share and accompanying warrant.
  • The company expects to receive aggregate gross proceeds of approximately $7.5 million.
  • The warrants will have an exercise price of $1.10 per share, will be exercisable immediately and will expire five years from the issuance date.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about May 8.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from this offering primarily for working capital and general corporate purposes, including for research and development and manufacturing scale-up.
  • TOVX -8.82% premarket to $1.24.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: SolarEdge launches new solar-powered EV charging solution
data.description:
• SolarEdge Technologies (NASDAQ:SEDG) launched a solar-powered EV charging solution for businesses.
  • Available now, the new solution includes a new EV charger for businesses, powered and controlled by an energy management system introduced following Wevo Energy’s acquisition last year.
  • This solution joins the SolarEdge C&I ecosystem for commercial-scale solar and storage solutions.
  • Businesses with SolarEdge’s solar systems can now charge their EV fleet with ‘power-optimized’ solar energy to further reduce their energy bills.
  • SolarEdge ONE EV Charger is now available for commercial applications and is expected to be available for residential applications in Q3 2025.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Klaviyo rises after 'impressive' Q1, no macro issues keep analysts positive
data.description:
Shares of Klaviyo (NYSE:KVYO) jumped about 10% premarket on Wednesday after first quarter results and outlook, which saw largely positive views from analysts.

Needham maintained its Buy rating on Klaviyo — which provides a cloud-native platform for data storing — but cut the price target on the stock to $45 from $56.

Analysts led by Scott Berg said Klaviyo "reported impressive 1Q results led by upmarket execution and international traction continuing to drive recent outperformance." They added that Net Revenue Retention Rate, or NRR, stabilized as the company benefited from better than expected retention after the recent pricing changes while total and large customer totals continued to trend higher.

The analysts noted that a big highlight surrounded Klaviyo's new product offerings, with initial interest in Service (beta) providing management optimism, while commentary surrounding the average revenue per user, or ARPU, uplift from Marketing Analytics drove Needham's optimism for FY25 and FY26 growth prospects.

First quarter billings accelerated about 2 points, highlighting the up market sales strength. The company's management noted they have yet to see macro-related headwinds but did bake in some cautiousness yet increased financial year 2025 guidance more than the first quarter outperformance confirming the minimal impact.

Piper Sandler reaffirmed its Overweight rating on the stock and raised the price target to $40 from $35.

"Solid execution impressed us once again with the continuation of new product cross-sell and increasing mid-market customer mix, contributing to ARPC expansion that drove Q1 upside on 33% y/y growth (vs. 27% mid-point guide)," said Piper analysts led by Brent Bracelin.

The analysts added that this differentiated data-first platform operating at over $1B ARR scale with an expanding product set might not be immune to macro headwinds but does have several levers that can help insulate growth including — revenue generating software category with a compelling ROI making it one of the last budgets cut; increasing share gain offset; and new product cross-sell across SMS, service, and analytics into 169,000+ customer base.

Other growth levers include, international expansion driven by local language support, and increasing mix shift to mid-market and enterprise customers. The analysts added thate raised the price target on higher estimates (even after factoring in a higher level of conservatism).

Meanwhile, Stifel maintained its Buy rating on Klaviyo but cut the price target on the stock to $45 from $54.

"Kiaviyo reported a clean 1Q print and raised its oullook for the year. After a volatile quarter of headlines around tariffs and the potential impact to the retail/e-commerce space, the company noted that they've seen no material impact to their business thus far, and that based on customer feedback, guidance reflects some potential economic risk that's balanced with the strength of the business," said analysts led by J. Parker Lane.

The analysts added that mid/up-market momentum, international expansion, and SMS attach all trended in the right direction during the quarter, driving solid customer acquisition and consistent retention trends.

Lane and his team added that besides the macro and the factors noted above, they expect investors will remain focused on the initial uptake of Customer Hub and Marketing Analytics as the year progresses, despite immaterial revenue contributions being baked into the guidance this time.

Related stocks: HubSpot (HUBS) was largely flat, while Five9 (FIVN) rose about 2% premarket on Wednesday.

data.title: Super League announces 35% workforce reduction
data.description:
• Super League (NASDAQ:SLE) announced efforts to reduce its costs structure through a 35% workforce reduction and restructuring of management compensation. 
  • The measures, effective primarily as of April 14, 2025, and May 1st, 2025, respectively, are expected to reduce fiscal year operating costs by ~$2.7M, which should help SLE reach its targeted goal of being EBITDA positive in Q4 2025. 
  • Source: Press release
